jokes.text: I know how much you enjoy paperwork, but don't hover.
jokes.setup: Leslie knows Ron hates paperwork and bureaucracy
jokes.punchline: She sarcastically tells him not to hover over paperwork he despises
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor that plays on Ron's well-established hatred of government bureaucracy

jokes.text: ♪ Birthday, birthday, birthday ♪ ♪ it's your birthday ♪
jokes.setup: Leslie discovers Ron's birthday secret
jokes.punchline: She immediately breaks into an obnoxious birthday song
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie enthusiasm contrasted with Ron's privacy needs creates good cringe comedy

jokes.text: Nice bench. Is that new? / No, that's been there since the '90s.
jokes.setup: Leslie noticing a bench as if it's new
jokes.punchline: Ron's matter-of-fact response that it's been there for decades
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor showing Leslie's obliviousness and Ron's deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: Until a well-placed bribe to a gentleman at Baskin-Robbins revealed... Ron's birthday is on Friday.
jokes.setup: Leslie's elaborate investigation methods
jokes.punchline: All her spy work was undone by Ron getting free ice cream
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Leslie's obsessive nature and Ron's simple weakness for free ice cream

jokes.text: All for a free scoop of rum raisin. Was it worth it, Ron? Was it?
jokes.setup: Ron's privacy was compromised by ice cream
jokes.punchline: Leslie dramatically questions if the ice cream was worth his secret
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that maintains the absurd gravity Leslie places on birthday discovery

jokes.text: Birthdays were invented by Hallmark to sell cards.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining why he hates birthdays
jokes.punchline: Conspiracy theory about Hallmark inventing birthdays
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson anti-commercialism delivered with characteristic certainty

jokes.text: And that's coming from someone who has a Mercedes... With a Harman Kardon Logic 7 surround-sound system.
jokes.setup: Tom calling Eagleton rich snobs
jokes.punchline: He undermines his point by immediately bragging about his luxury car features
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom Haverford moment - criticizing wealth while showing off his own materialistic obsessions

jokes.text: Three words... Lindsay Carlisle Shay.
jokes.setup: Building up to reveal who's responsible
jokes.punchline: Counting three first names as 'three words'
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay joke with the pretentious three-name structure being treated as 'three words.'

jokes.text: and lost something else. What was it again? Oh, yeah. Her soul.
jokes.setup: Lindsay lost weight and fixed her nose
jokes.punchline: Leslie delivers the dramatic revelation that she lost her soul
jokes.explanation: Well-structured dramatic reveal with good buildup and Leslie's characteristic moral indignation

jokes.text: Our side is this scrappy piece of land where kids go to smash fluorescent light tubes. But it has a lot of heart.
jokes.setup: Defending Pawnee's side of the park
jokes.punchline: Admits it's where kids commit minor vandalism but insists it has heart
jokes.explanation: Leslie's eternal optimism even when describing obvious problems with Pawnee

jokes.text: That's what people always say when something sucks.
jokes.setup: Leslie says the park 'has a lot of heart'
jokes.punchline: Ann immediately deflates the euphemism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan observation that punctures Leslie's relentless positivity with brutal honesty

jokes.text: although on average, we are several inches shorter and 80 pounds heavier.
jokes.setup: Leslie insisting Pawneeans are as good as Eagletonians
jokes.punchline: She immediately undercuts her point with unflattering statistics
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie move - trying to be positive while accidentally revealing embarrassing facts

jokes.text: Leslie has a lot of qualities I find horrifying. But the worst one by far is how thoughtful she can be.
jokes.setup: Ron listing Leslie's horrifying qualities
jokes.punchline: The 'worst' quality is actually being thoughtful
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - his gruff exterior cracking to reveal genuine affection disguised as complaint

jokes.text: Why don't we build a fence around their fence? [...] so if they needed to get to their fence for maintenance and whatnot, their pants might get caught.
jokes.setup: Andy suggests building a fence around their fence
jokes.punchline: His logic is that it would cause minor pants-catching inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Andy logic - elaborate solution with minimal and oddly specific consequences

jokes.text: I'm just gonna suggest one more time that we burn it down. But whatever you guys think.
jokes.setup: Andy has been suggesting arson
jokes.punchline: He casually suggests arson again like it's a reasonable option
jokes.explanation: Good callback that maintains Andy's casual approach to extreme solutions

jokes.text: Okay, I-I'm just gonna suggest one more time that we burn it down. But whatever you guys think.
jokes.setup: Previous arson suggestions being ignored
jokes.punchline: Politely suggesting arson again with casual dismissal
jokes.explanation: Nice callback with polite persistence on violent solution. The casual tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Also, what about your neon gangster fedora hats?
jokes.setup: Already ordering inflatable saxophones
jokes.punchline: Adding even more ridiculous party supplies
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with the specific 'neon gangster fedora' being perfectly absurd party gear.

jokes.text: Hi, Dan. - Ben. - It's not important. It won't come up again.
jokes.setup: Lindsay gets Ben's name wrong
jokes.punchline: Ben awkwardly accepts the dismissal of his identity
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Lindsay's dismissive attitude and Ben's awkward acceptance

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sorry. It's just, nursing... You must be so tired. It's sad.
jokes.setup: Ann mentions she's a nurse
jokes.punchline: Lindsay condescendingly pities her career choice
jokes.explanation: Effective character moment showing Lindsay's snobbish condescension

jokes.text: Pawnee is and always will be a dirty, little nightmare from which you will never wake up.
jokes.setup: Lindsay dismissing Pawnee
jokes.punchline: Dramatically declares it an inescapable nightmare
jokes.explanation: Memorable villain line with good dramatic flair and metaphor

jokes.text: What? I'm not asleep. I'm awake. I'm wide awake, and I got my eyes on you.
jokes.setup: Lindsay's nightmare metaphor
jokes.punchline: Leslie takes it literally and protests she's not sleeping
jokes.explanation: Leslie missing the metaphor creates good character-based humor

jokes.text: That's what I would've said if I had thought of it in the moment. What did I say instead?
jokes.setup: Leslie in confessional reflecting on the confrontation
jokes.punchline: Admitting she had a better comeback only in hindsight
jokes.explanation: Relatable moment of thinking of the perfect comeback too late. Good self-awareness from Leslie.

jokes.text: They had a valet.
jokes.setup: Discussing Eagleton's public forum venue
jokes.punchline: Tom is impressed by the valet service at a government meeting
jokes.explanation: Good Tom moment showing his focus on luxury amenities even in government settings

jokes.text: What, did you just come from the stables? - Yes. I was just at the stables.
jokes.setup: Tom mocks Lindsay's outfit as stable-appropriate
jokes.punchline: She was actually at the stables, deflating his insult
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that undercuts Tom's attempted insult with unexpected truth

jokes.text: There is no street parking at my house. My house is not even on a street.
jokes.setup: April asking about parking at Ron's house
jokes.punchline: Ron revealing his house isn't even on a street
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson isolation escalated to absurd levels. The progression from no parking to no street is brilliant.

jokes.text: These were for a sick child at the hospital. - Ah.
jokes.setup: Ron discovers April destroyed birthday balloons
jokes.punchline: April deadpan claims they were for a sick child, Ron accepts this
jokes.explanation: Perfect April deadpan lie that's both absurd and delivered with complete conviction

jokes.text: Oh, you can't, because you broke your arm climbing that fence. - Uh, I can wave with this arm. - No, you can't. Both your arms are broken.
jokes.setup: Leslie using Joey as prop for her argument
jokes.punchline: She insists both his arms are broken when he demonstrates otherwise
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Leslie's desperation to make her point override reality

jokes.text: Isn't she trying her hardest? So cute and so good.
jokes.setup: Lindsay commenting on Leslie's efforts
jokes.punchline: The patronizing tone treating Leslie like a child
jokes.explanation: Perfectly condescending delivery that treats Leslie like a well-meaning but incompetent child.

jokes.text: She kidnapped me from work, and then she took me to that place, Senor Vega's [...] And then there was a bouncy castle. Did you know they made those for adults?
jokes.setup: Andrew describing his surprise birthday party
jokes.punchline: Escalating list of increasingly elaborate and childish party elements
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Leslie's over-the-top party planning and Andrew's innocent enthusiasm

jokes.text: Did you guys get your public forum gift bag? There's an iPod touch in here.
jokes.setup: Eagleton's excessive amenities
jokes.punchline: They give away expensive electronics at town meetings
jokes.explanation: Good detail showing Eagleton's wealth excess through Tom's materialistic lens

jokes.text: Unless... That's exactly what she wants me to do.
jokes.setup: Ron considering leaving early to avoid birthday party
jokes.punchline: He realizes this might be part of Leslie's plan
jokes.explanation: Good paranoia moment showing Ron's respect for Leslie's strategic thinking

jokes.text: And if you have any job openings, maybe you should let me know about 'em. [...] I'm sick of being treated like I'm not willing to relocate to Eagleton, because I am!
jokes.setup: Tom confronting Lindsay
jokes.punchline: His anger turns into a desperate job pitch
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment where Tom's loyalty crumbles into opportunism mid-speech

jokes.text: You might have a fancy car and a mahogany purse, or whatever rich people have,
jokes.setup: Tom listing rich people things
jokes.punchline: The made-up 'mahogany purse' and admission he doesn't know what rich people have
jokes.explanation: Good Tom moment showing his aspirational but confused view of wealth

jokes.text: You might have a fancy car and a mahogany purse, or whatever rich people have
jokes.setup: Leslie listing Lindsay's fancy possessions
jokes.punchline: She doesn't know what rich people have beyond cars and 'mahogany purses'
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie moment showing her middle-class assumptions about wealth

jokes.text: These waffles make great dog laxatives. [...] Don't you dare feed that waffle to that dog to get it to poop.
jokes.setup: Lindsay claims JJ's waffles aren't for her
jokes.punchline: She reveals they're used as dog laxatives, Leslie's horrified reaction
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that makes Lindsay even more villainous through waffle desecration

jokes.text: Sambuca need to make? There you go.
jokes.setup: Lindsay about to feed the waffle to her dog
jokes.punchline: The dog immediately pooping after eating the waffle
jokes.explanation: Immediate payoff to the laxative setup. Physical comedy with the dog's instant reaction to the waffle.

jokes.text: Did G.B. Shaw write that for you? - Did G.B. Shaw write your stupid fartface?
jokes.setup: Lindsay makes literary insult
jokes.punchline: Leslie's comeback awkwardly repurposes the structure
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Leslie's inability to match Lindsay's sophistication

jokes.text: Can I offer you anything... herbal tea, Greek yogurt?
jokes.setup: Officer offering amenities to prisoners
jokes.punchline: Luxury items being offered in jail
jokes.explanation: Good contrast showing Eagleton's luxury extends even to their jail. The specific items are perfectly bougie.

jokes.text: I love you like a father who's not that much older than me... like a young Uncle... or like, uh, you were my camp counselor
jokes.setup: Andrew trying to explain his relationship with Ron
jokes.punchline: Increasingly awkward analogies that don't quite work
jokes.explanation: Good Andrew rambling that captures his earnest confusion about relationships

jokes.text: Leslie assigned me to the kidnap squad.
jokes.setup: Andrew accidentally revealing party plans
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of being on a 'kidnap squad'
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor with perfect economy - the casual mention of kidnapping is perfectly Andrew

jokes.text: Ann, put these scones in your bra. Let's go.
jokes.setup: Leslie having an idea in jail
jokes.punchline: Her plan involves smuggling prison scones in Ann's bra
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie absurdity - treating jail amenity theft as important mission with military precision

jokes.text: That was just a list of ways to mess with you. - She do 'em all? - She did indeed.
jokes.setup: Revealing April's 'party planning' was actually harassment
jokes.punchline: April successfully completed every item on the harassment list
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that recontextualizes April's earlier behavior as intentional harassment

jokes.text: Next year, your birthday party is gonna be a rager.
jokes.setup: Leslie saying this is a one-time low-key birthday
jokes.punchline: She immediately threatens an even bigger party next year
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - can't help but escalate even while trying to be considerate

jokes.text: This, by the way, is a one-time-only situation. Next year, your birthday party is gonna be a rager.
jokes.setup: Leslie giving Ron a quiet birthday celebration
jokes.punchline: Her threat/promise that next year will be an over-the-top party
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - can't help but threaten more elaborate celebrations. Shows she'll never truly change.
